title: Fragments: The Ship of State
description: Alcaeus of Mytilene: the poet who gave Western literature the metaphor of the ship of state.
introduction: Alcaeus of Mytilene, contemporary of Sappho, invented the metaphor of the ship of state tossed in a storm — an image that has been used by every political tradition since, from Plato to Lincoln.

translation.original_text: ἀσυννέτημμι τὼν ἀνέμων στάσιν· τὸ μὲν γὰρ ἔνθεν κῦμα κυλίνδεται, τὸ δ᾿ ἔνθεν.
translation.text: I cannot understand the direction of the winds: one wave rolls from this side, another from that.
